description: Mega PRD: fix graph bugs, improve merge UI, add missing context menu actions, graph refresh after operations, current branch indicator, profiles, SSH keys, commit signing, GitHub/GitLab integration.

userStories.title: Current Branch Visual Indicator in Commit Graph
userStories.description: As a user, I want to clearly see which branch I am currently on in the commit graph.
acceptanceCriteria: The current branch's pill badge in the commit graph has a distinct visual treatment: bold border, brighter color, or a checkmark/dot icon prefix
The HEAD commit row has a stronger left accent border (3px var(--accent)) to distinguish it from selected commits
If the current branch label is off-screen (scrolled away), show a small floating indicator at the top of the graph: 'HEAD: branchname'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CommitGraph.tsx renders ref badges. The HEAD ref already has a CircleDot icon. Enhancement: make it more prominent. The current branch is available from git status data.

userStories.title: Graph Refresh After Git Operations
userStories.description: As a user, I want the commit graph to automatically refresh after merge, rebase, checkout, commit, push, pull, and other git operations.
acceptanceCriteria: After a successful merge (MergeDialog), the commit graph reloads
After a successful commit (StatusPanel), the graph reloads
After checkout, push, pull, fetch, rebase, cherry-pick, revert — graph reloads
The reload preserves scroll position where possible
Use the existing onRepoChanged file watcher — verify it fires after all operations
If watcher doesn't fire for some operations, trigger manual refresh via a callback or event
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The app has an onRepoChanged file watcher. Check if MergeDialog calls onRefresh after completion. CommitGraph listens to onRepoChanged — verify the listener works. MergeDialog should dispatch a refresh event or call a callback prop after merge completes.

userStories.title: Add Merge to Commit Right-Click Context Menu
userStories.description: As a user, I want to merge a branch by right-clicking a commit in the graph.
acceptanceCriteria: Right-clicking a commit that has branch refs shows 'Merge [branchname] into [current branch]'
Clicking opens MergeDialog pre-filled with the selected branch
If commit has no branch refs or is on current branch, merge option not shown
After merge completes, graph refreshes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Commit context menu in CommitGraph.tsx. Branch ref context menus already have merge. Add merge to the COMMIT context menu when refs are present.

userStories.title: Redesign MergeDialog — Clean, Polished UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want the merge dialog to look polished and not crammed.
acceptanceCriteria: Proper modal overlay (centered, max-width 480px, proper padding var(--space-lg))
Title: 'Merge into [current branch]'
Branch selector: searchable dropdown showing branches to merge from
Preview: ahead/behind counts, number of commits to merge
Options: --no-ff checkbox, --squash checkbox
Merge button: deep blue (#3b82f6), white text, font-weight 600
Cancel button: subtle secondary style
Loading state with spinner during merge
Error state with clear message
Success: brief confirmation then auto-close
Uses MergeDialog.module.css with design tokens, not global branch-dialog classes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: MergeDialog.tsx (360 lines) needs visual redesign. Currently uses branch-dialog global CSS. Rewrite CSS module. The merge IPC (mergePreview, merge) already exists.

userStories.title: Enhanced Commit Context Menu
userStories.description: As a user, I want the commit right-click menu to have all GitKraken-like actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Full menu: Checkout, Cherry-pick, Revert, Reset Soft, Reset Mixed, Reset Hard (with warning), Create branch, Create tag, Copy SHA, Copy commit message
If commit has branch refs: Merge [branch] into current, Rebase current onto [branch]
Separators between action groups
Dangerous actions (Reset Hard) styled in red
All repo-modifying actions trigger graph refresh
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Existing menu has most items. Add: copy commit message, split reset into soft/mixed/hard, add merge/rebase when branch refs present. Group with separators.

userStories.title: Profiles — Create, Edit, Switch
userStories.description: As a user, I want named profiles with author name and email to switch between identities.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings panel has a 'Profiles' section
Create profile: name, author name (user.name), author email (user.email)
Edit and delete profiles
Default profile selection
Profiles stored in electron-store config.json
Switching profiles runs git config user.name and user.email on current repo
Profile indicator in status bar shows active profile
Quick profile selector in toolbar or status bar
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Store in electron-store: { profiles: [{ id, name, authorName, authorEmail, isDefault }] }. Add IPC for CRUD. Apply via git config commands. SettingsPanel.tsx already has sections — add Profiles.

userStories.title: SSH Key Management
userStories.description: As a user, I want to manage SSH keys from within the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings has SSH Keys section
List existing keys from ~/.ssh/
Generate new key: Ed25519 or RSA, name, optional passphrase
Uses ssh-keygen via child_process
Copy public key to clipboard button
Test connection: ssh -T git@github.com with result display
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Filesystem operations on ~/.ssh/. Use child_process.exec for ssh-keygen. Read directory for key listing. All in main process via IPC.

userStories.title: Commit Signing Configuration
userStories.description: As a user, I want to configure GPG or SSH commit signing per profile.
acceptanceCriteria: Profile editor has Signing section: None, GPG, SSH
GPG: dropdown of available keys (gpg --list-keys), selected stored in profile
SSH: dropdown of SSH keys, selected stored in profile
Applying profile sets git config commit.gpgsign, user.signingkey, gpg.format
Existing signature display in commit details works correctly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: GPG listing via existing getAvailableGpgKeys IPC. SSH signing: gpg.format=ssh, user.signingkey=path/to/key.pub. Apply via git config on profile switch.

userStories.title: GitHub Integration — OAuth Login
userStories.description: As a user, I want to log in to GitHub from GitSlop.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings has GitHub section
Login uses GitHub OAuth device flow or Personal Access Token input
Token stored securely (electron safeStorage or encrypted in electron-store)
Shows logged-in user: avatar, username
Logout clears token
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: GitHub device flow: POST /login/device/code, show code to user, poll /login/oauth/access_token. Or simpler: let user paste a PAT. Store with safeStorage. GET /user for user info.

userStories.title: GitHub — View and Create Pull Requests
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view and create GitHub PRs from within GitSlop.
acceptanceCriteria: When GitHub remote detected, show Pull Requests section in sidebar
List open PRs: title, number, author, status, CI checks
Click PR to see details: description, comments, review status, files changed
Create PR button: dialog with title, description, base branch, head branch
After creating, show PR URL with link to open in browser
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Parse owner/repo from remote URL. GitHub API: GET /repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls, POST /repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls. New PullRequests component for sidebar.

userStories.title: GitLab Integration — Login and Merge Requests
userStories.description: As a user, I want to log in to GitLab and view/create merge requests.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings has GitLab section with instance URL field (gitlab.com default, supports self-hosted)
Login via Personal Access Token (user pastes token)
Token stored securely
Shows logged-in user info
List open MRs when GitLab remote detected
Create MR dialog: title, description, source/target branch
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: GitLab API: /api/v4. PAT simpler than OAuth for self-hosted. Parse project from remote URL. Reuse PR list UI components.

userStories.title: Interactive Rebase UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to interactively rebase commits with a visual UI for reordering, squashing, and editing.
acceptanceCriteria: Right-click a branch or commit > 'Interactive rebase' opens the rebase UI
Shows list of commits to be rebased with drag-to-reorder
Each commit has a dropdown: pick, squash, fixup, edit, drop, reword
Preview of the result before executing
Execute button runs git rebase --interactive with the specified actions
Progress indicator during rebase
Conflict resolution integration (existing ConflictResolver)
Abort button if rebase goes wrong
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The rebaseInteractive IPC handler already exists. The UI needs: commit list with drag-reorder, action dropdowns, and progress tracking. RebaseDialog.tsx exists but may need enhancement.
